llm_reasoning: The location is poorly suited for maize. The strongest limiting factor is the extremely low mean temperature (5.59°C), indicating a very short and cool growing season, which will severely hinder growth and maturity. The acidic soil pH (5.12) is another significant limitation, restricting nutrient availability. The strongest positive factor is the adequate annual rainfall (667.59mm) and good clay content (18.7%), which support water retention. Operational risks include poor germination, slow development, failure to mature before frost, and nutrient deficiencies/toxicity due to low pH. Mitigation requires selecting very early-maturing, cold-tolerant varieties and significant soil liming to raise pH.
